join_order.rationale: Customer is smallest post-filter (300K). Build custkey hash set; probe orders. 20% of orders match → 1.44M qualifying orders.
Filtered+joined orders (1.44M) is much smaller than filtered lineitem (31.2M). Build compact hash map on qualifying o_orderkey. Probe from lineitem with bloom filter pre-check.

filter_pushdown.index: orders_orderdate_zonemap — skip blocks with block_min >= 9204, removes ~52% of blocks
lineitem_shipdate_zonemap — skip blocks with block_max <= 9204, removes ~48% of blocks

data_structures.orders_join_map: compact_hash_map<int32_t, {int32_t o_orderdate, int32_t o_shippriority}>(pre_sized=2880000) — 1.44M qualifying orders, ~34MB, maps o_orderkey→(o_orderdate, o_shippriority)
data_structures.orders_bloom_filter: bloom_filter(2MB, 3-hash) — built from 1.44M o_orderkey values; ~1% FP rate; pre-filters 31.2M lineitem probes, eliminating ~95% of non-matching rows before hash table lookup
data_structures.aggregation: partitioned_hash_map(32 partitions) — thread_local per partition, key=(l_orderkey:int32), value={revenue:double, o_orderdate:int32, o_shippriority:int32}; ~1.44M groups at ~20 bytes each = ~29MB per partition set; merge after parallel phase
data_structures.topk_heap: max_heap<{revenue:double, o_orderdate:int32, o_shippriority:int32, l_orderkey:int32}>(size=10) — O(n log 10) = O(n) over 1.44M groups

phase_1_customer.description: Parallel scan of c_mktsegment + c_custkey. Each thread accumulates thread-local vector of qualifying custkeys. Single merge into shared compact_hash_set after all threads complete.

phase_2_orders.description: Parallel morsel-driven scan of o_orderdate + o_custkey + o_orderkey + o_shippriority. Zone map consulted once per block (sequential, single-threaded block-skip), then qualifying blocks distributed as morsels to 32 threads. Each thread filters rows (o_orderdate<9204, custkey set probe) and inserts into the shared compact_hash_map using lock_free_cas or partitioned insert.
phase_2_orders.synchronization: partitioned_no_contention — hash o_orderkey to 32 partitions; each thread owns its partition segment during build; final map merged into single structure

phase_3_lineitem.description: Parallel morsel-driven scan of l_shipdate + l_orderkey + l_extendedprice + l_discount. Zone map skip applied before morsel distribution. Each morsel: (1) filter l_shipdate>9204, (2) bloom filter check on l_orderkey, (3) probe orders_join_map, (4) accumulate into thread-local aggregation map partitioned by hash(l_orderkey)%32.
phase_3_lineitem.synchronization: thread_local_merge — 32 thread-local agg maps, merge at end; each thread owns disjoint partitions so no locking needed

phase_4_topk.description: Sequential merge of 32 partition agg maps into single result vector (~1.44M entries), then std::partial_sort for top-10 by (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC).

index_usage.purpose: skip_blocks_outside_range — skip blocks with block_min >= 9204; eliminates ~52% of orders I/O (trailing date blocks in ascending-sorted file)
index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks=150, then per block: {int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size} (12 bytes each)

index_usage.purpose: skip_blocks_outside_range — skip blocks with block_max <= 9204; eliminates ~48% of lineitem I/O (leading date blocks in ascending-sorted file)
index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks=600, then per block: {int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size} (12 bytes each)

index_usage.purpose: NOT_USED — sequential scan of orders with zone map is superior for 48% selectivity; random index lookups on HDD would be slower than sequential zone-map-guided scan
index_usage.rationale: Index lookup useful only for <5% selectivity on HDD

index_usage.purpose: NOT_USED — probing 31.2M lineitem rows sequentially via zone-map scan is faster than 1.44M random index lookups on HDD; bloom filter handles sparsity
index_usage.rationale: 52% of lineitem qualifies by date; sequential scan + bloom filter is more cache-friendly than random multi-value index lookups

scan_details.customer: fused_scan(c_mktsegment, c_custkey) — single pass, apply dict-code filter inline, collect custkeys
scan_details.orders: zone_map_guided_fused_scan(o_orderdate, o_custkey, o_orderkey, o_shippriority) — load zone map once, skip blocks with block_min>=9204, fuse date filter + custkey probe + map insert in single pass
scan_details.lineitem: zone_map_guided_fused_scan(l_shipdate, l_orderkey, l_extendedprice, l_discount) — load zone map once, skip blocks with block_max<=9204, fuse: shipdate filter → bloom check → hash probe → aggregate

memory_details.customer_phase: streaming_sequential — 1.5M rows × 2 columns × 1-4 bytes = ~7.5MB, fully L3-resident
memory_details.orders_phase: streaming_sequential — scans ~7.7M qualifying blocks (~60% of file); hash map builds in RAM (34MB)
memory_details.lineitem_phase: streaming_sequential for scan columns; random_access_with_prefetch for orders hash map probe (34MB, fits in L3 at 44MB); thread-local agg maps stay L3-warm per partition
memory_details.aggregation_phase: random_access — ~1.44M groups × 20 bytes = 29MB total agg state; with 32 partitions each thread's partition ~900KB fits in L2/L3

phase_breakdown.phase_2_orders_scan_build: ~15ms (zone map skips 52% → ~7.2M rows scanned; 1.44M map inserts; parallel morsel-driven)
phase_breakdown.phase_3_lineitem_scan_probe_agg: ~65ms (zone map skips 48% → 31.2M rows; bloom filter eliminates ~95% probes; 5.76M hash lookups into 34MB map; 32-thread parallel agg)

estimated_cost.bottleneck_notes: Lineitem phase dominates: 31.2M rows × 4 columns × 4-8 bytes = ~750MB sequential reads, amortized across 32 threads. Bloom filter is critical — without it, 31.2M hash table probes into 34MB map would cause L3 thrashing. With bloom: only ~5.76M actual probes needed.
